LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT, WALGREEN CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH C.C.P. 437(C)(G). THE ORDER ORDER SHALL INCLUDE THAT PLAINTIFF, MR. GRAVOLET MAY SEEK RELIEF FROM THIS RULING WITHIN 14 DAYS OF RECEIVING THE ORDER. THE COURT WILL NOT ENTER JUDGMENT UNTIL THE EXPIRATION OF THE 14 DAYS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF WESLEY ALEXANDER GRAVOLET, AN INDIVIDUAL TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Patrick M. Callahan of the law offices of Burnham Brown (510-444-6800) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Court inquires and hears from the moving party as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Counsel indicates that he has been in contact with plaintiff regarding notice that counsel for plaintiff is no longer operating as a law firm and is currently in bankruptcy proceedings.

DEFENDANT, WALGREEN CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with C.C.P. 437(c)(g). The order order shall include that Plaintiff, Mr. Gravolet may seek relief from this ruling within 14 days of receiving the order. The Court will not enter judgment until the expiration of the 14 days.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Attendance At Deposition And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT WALGREEN COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Attendance At Deposition And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED. Unopposed. Sanctions in the amount of $1,100 are GRANTED. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim. The proposed order may be submitted electronically, in Word (.doc) format and by Email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (DISC302)
